original_topic: IAM Multi-Provider Authentication — OAuth 2.0, OIDC, Telegram, Passkeys, token management, session management, account linking, DDD patterns

external_systems: Redis (session cache, permission cache, token blacklist)

key_questions: How do market leaders (Auth0, Keycloak, Supabase) model the Identity aggregate with multiple authentication providers?
What are best practices for JWT access token structure (claims, lifetime, signing algorithm) and refresh token rotation?
How should account linking work when a user authenticates via a new provider but already has an identity (email match, explicit linking)?
What session management patterns are used in production (sliding expiry, concurrent session limits, device binding)?
How do IAM systems implement multi-factor authentication and step-up auth in a clean architecture?
What domain events should an Identity bounded context emit (and what are the consumers)?
How do production systems handle Telegram Mini App authentication securely (initData validation, freshness, replay prevention)?
What are the OWASP and NIST SP 800-63B requirements for token storage, credential hashing, and session management?
How do passkeys/WebAuthn integrate into a multi-provider IAM system alongside traditional auth methods?
What DDD/Clean Architecture patterns are recommended for modeling IAM (bounded context boundaries, aggregate design, CQRS for auth)?

existing_system.stack: Python 3.14 / FastAPI / SQLAlchemy 2.1 (async) / Alembic / Dishka DI / PostgreSQL / Redis / RabbitMQ / attrs / Pydantic v2 / structlog
relevant_models: Identity (aggregate: id, type, account_type, is_active, deactivated_at/by)
Session (entity: refresh_token_hash, ip_address, user_agent, is_revoked, expires_at, activated_roles)
TelegramCredentials (telegram_id, first_name, last_name, username, language_code, is_premium, photo_url)
constraints: Domain layer uses only attrs + stdlib (no framework imports)
Cross-module communication via transactional outbox only
Data Mapper pattern — ORM models never leak into domain
JWT HS256 access tokens (15min TTL), opaque refresh tokens (30d TTL, SHA-256 hashed in DB)
Refresh token rotation with reuse detection
RBAC with cache-aside permission resolution (Redis + CTE)

search_keywords: JWT access token refresh token rotation
